revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:地蔵塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{Infobox ancient site}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 地蔵塚古墳 |image = [[File:Jizozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |alternate_name = |所属 = |location = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}[[:en:笛吹市|笛吹市]]八代町南（字塚ノ越） |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 36|coordinates = 52.25 |coordinates = 138|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 34.40 |omitted = JP-19 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = 直径35m
高さ5m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = |陪塚 = |built = [[6世紀]]末-[[7世紀]]初頭 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 笛吹市指定史跡「地蔵塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Yamanashi |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 地蔵塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Jizozuka Kofun''' (地蔵塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Fuefuki|en|Fuefuki|qid=Q849434|lt=Fuefuki|1=Fuefuki}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a circular kofun Historic Site1 as a municipal historic site by Fuefuki City. == Overview == The Jizozuka Kofun is a large circular Kofun built on the central area of the Asakawa alluvial fan in the southeastern [[Kōfu Basin|Kofu Basin]], at an elevation of 305 meters. The kofun is mentioned in the ''[[Kai Kokushi]]'' as "Jizozuka" and is known to have been opened in ancient times. Currently, statues of the deity Jizo (standing and sitting) are enshrined in its stone chamber. No archaeological excavation has been conducted to date. The mound is circular, with a diameter of 35 meters and a height of 5 meters. The burial chamber is a {{ill|Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|zh|橫穴式石室|ko|굴식_돌방무덤}} (horizontal stone chamber) opening to the southwest. The chamber measures 10.1 meters in length, Ranking among the largest in Yamanashi Prefecture and being the largest in the typeer Yatsushiro Town area. Details of the burial goods are unknown. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the late [[Kofun period]], around the end of the {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} to the beginning of the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a historic site by the typeer Yatsushiro Town in {{ill|1973|en|1973|qid=Q2477|lt=1973|1=1973}} (currently under Fuefuki City). == burial == [[File:Jizozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|Overhead view of the stone chamber]] [[File:Jizozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Unfolded view of the stone chamber]] The burial chamber is a {{ill|Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|zh|橫穴式石室|ko|굴식_돌방무덤}} (horizontal stone chamber) opening to the southwest. The chamber area are as follows: * Total length: 10.1 meters * burial chamber: Length 6 meters, width 2.5 meters (at the back wall) * Passage: Length 4.1 meters, width 1.6 meters (at the passage section), height 1.3 meters (at the entrance) The back wall of the chamber consists of large stones stacked in two horizontal layers, with gaps filled using smaller stones. The side walls are made of large natural stones arranged in roughly four courses with prominent overhangs. A columnar natural stone, measuring 0.7 meters in width and 1 meter in height, serves as the "sleeve stone." Five ceiling stones cover the chamber, with the two nearest the back wall lying nearly horizontal and sloping downward toward the entrance without typeing steps. The construction of the stone chamber resembles that of the nearby [[Ubazuka Kofun]] and [[Kamunazuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Kofu|Kofu]], suggesting close stylistic connections. Jizozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward back wall) Jizozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) Jizozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) Jizozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) Jizozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance-1.jpg|Entrance Jizozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funcho.jpg|Mound top == designation1 == === Municipal designation1 === * Historic Site: ''' Jizozuka Kofun - Designated February 20, 1973. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Intypeational sign at the site * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=Jizozuka Kofun|title=Yamanashi Prefectural History|volume=Source Materials, Vol. 1: Prehistoric and Ancient builts|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|pages=|ref={{Harv|Yamanashi Prefecture History, Source Materials Vol. 1|1998}}}} == Related Sites == {{Commonscat|Jizozuka Kofun (Fuefuki)}} * [[Ubazuka Kofun]] * [[Kamunazuka Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=101315394|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Jizozuka Kofun (Fuefuki)}} [[ja:地蔵塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{wikidata link|Q121856624}} [[Category:History of Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
